# Copilot Agent Instructions

## Project Overview

**ngbs-icon** is a TypeScript Node.js library and CLI for communicating with **NGBS iCON** radiant floor heating/cooling controllers over a local network. It implements the controller's proprietary JSON/TCP service protocol.

- **npm package**: `ngbs-icon`
- **CLI binary**: `ngbs_icon`
- **GitHub repo**: https://github.com/molnarg/ngbs-icon

## Tech Stack

- **Language**: TypeScript (strict, targeting Node 20 via `@tsconfig/node20`)
- **Runtime**: Node.js 20, zero runtime dependencies
- **Build**: `npx tsc` — compiles `src/**/*.ts` → `dist/`
- **No test framework** is configured; `npm test` exits with an error

## Project Structure

```
src/
  client.ts          # Public TypeScript interfaces (NgbsIconClient, NgbsIconState, etc.)
  service_client.ts  # NgbsIconServiceClient — the only concrete implementation
  url.ts             # connect(address) factory: parses service://SYSID@host URLs
  discovery.ts       # getSysId(), isNgbsHost(), isNgbsMacAddress() helpers
  ngbs_icon.ts       # CLI entry point (bin: ngbs_icon)
  index.ts           # Re-exports public API
dist/                # Compiled JS + .d.ts (gitignored, regenerated by tsc)
```

## Architecture

### Protocol

All communication happens over **TCP port 7992** using newline-terminated JSON frames. Each request is a single JSON object; the controller responds with a single JSON object.

Key protocol fields:
- `SYSID` — controller serial number (required in most requests; printed on the device; can also be fetched via `RELOAD: 6`)
- `RELOAD` — special command codes:
  - `3` → fetch full state including config (`CFG`)
  - `6` → fetch SYSID only
  - `7` → trigger software update
  - `8` → trigger restart (software reload, not reboot)
- `DP` — map of thermostat data, keyed by `"iconId.thermostatId"` (e.g. `"1.1"`)
- `CE` / `HC` — master ECO / heating-vs-cooling mode flags (0/1)

Thermostat wire fields (under `DP[id]`):

| Wire field | Meaning |
|-----------|---------|
| `ON` | Thermostat active |
| `LIVE` | Online/reachable |
| `TEMP` | Current temperature |
| `RH` | Relative humidity |
| `DEW` | Dew point |
| `DWP` | Dew protection active |
| `FROST` | Frost protection active |
| `OUT` | Valve open |
| `CE` | ECO mode |
| `CEF` | ECO follows master |
| `HC` | Cooling mode |
| `PL` | Parental lock |
| `TPR` | Time program active |
| `SP` | Set-point (current mode) |
| `XAH` / `XAC` | Comfort heating / cooling target |
| `ECOH` / `ECOC` | ECO heating / cooling target |
| `LIM` | Adjustment limit (±°C around midpoint) |
| `DXA` | Midpoint |
| `ZEB` | Heating/cooling diff |
| `ECO` (on DP) | ECO diff |
| `DXH` / `DXC` | Floor heating / cooling offset |
| `NAME` | Room name |

### Client Interface (`src/client.ts`)

Defines the public `NgbsIconClient` interface and associated data types. **Do not add implementation logic here** — it is documentation and the API contract.

### Service Client (`src/service_client.ts`)

`NgbsIconServiceClient` is the sole implementation of `NgbsIconClient`. Key design points:

- Every TCP request opens a new socket (short-lived, 2 s timeout).
- State-changing methods (e.g. `setThermostatTarget`, `setEco`, `setCooling`) call `waitForState()` after the mutation to poll until the controller stabilizes and valves have actuated. This can take up to ~10 s.
- `valvesSettled()` checks per-relay config (`CFG.ICONx.RELAY.Ry.HEAT/COOL`) to skip disabled relays.
- `parseState()` maps raw wire fields to the typed `NgbsIconState` / `NgbsIconThermostat` structures.

### URL Factory (`src/url.ts`)

`connect(address)` accepts `service://SYSID@host[:port]` URLs and returns an `NgbsIconServiceClient`. Throws if the protocol is unknown or SYSID is missing.

### Discovery (`src/discovery.ts`)

- `getSysId(host)` — fetches SYSID via `RELOAD: 6`; returns `''` for pre-2023 firmware.
- `isNgbsHost(host)` — probes whether a host responds as an NGBS controller.
- `isNgbsMacAddress(mac)` — checks against known NGBS MAC address prefixes (useful for ARP-based scanning).

### CLI (`src/ngbs_icon.ts`)

Parses `process.argv` manually (no CLI framework). If a bare IP is given (no `service://` prefix) it auto-fetches the SYSID first. Outputs JSON to stdout.

## Build & Development

```bash
npx tsc          # build (outputs to dist/)
npx tsc --watch  # incremental watch mode
```

No linter or formatter is configured. Follow the existing code style: 4-space indentation, single quotes, async/await throughout.

## Known Issues & Quirks

- Some older controller firmware versions embed `0000` in JSON responses, which causes `JSON.parse` to fail. A workaround is to strip those sequences before parsing.
- Pre-2023 firmware (below version 1079) requires the SYSID in **every** request, including the SYSID-discovery request, and will return an error instead of the SYSID. `getSysId()` handles this by returning `''`.
- The `midpoint` field on `NgbsIconThermostat` is derived from controller-level midpoints; individual thermostats cannot report their own midpoints back.

## Conventions

- All public types live in `client.ts`; implementation stays in `service_client.ts`.
- Prefer `async/await` over raw Promise chains.
- TCP sockets are opened and closed per-request; do not keep persistent connections.
- When adding new settable properties, follow the pattern: send the mutation, then call `waitForState()` with appropriate required/optional conditions before returning.
